prompt: The equality check prints false even though both arrays contain the same values. What is the bug?
choices: int[] does not support equals() — use double[] instead
== and .equals() on arrays compare object references, not contents; use Arrays.equals(a, b)
Both arrays must be sorted before comparison
equals() requires that both arrays have the same variable name
explanation: For arrays, == compares references (are they the same object?), and .equals() is inherited from Object and also compares references. To compare contents use Arrays.equals(a, b), which returns true for {1,2,3} vs {1,2,3}.
